findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "BrowseWixRESTDocsMenu" is 5013 chars, far longer than this server's median (931). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Browse the Wix REST API documentation menu hierarchy. Alternative to SearchWixRESTDocumentation - use this to explore and discover APIs by navigating the menu s…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "CallWixSiteAPI" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Call apis on site "CodeStringers - Zoho Services" (https://www.codestringers.com/_api/mcp). Use this to perform an action on visitor's behalf, for example, quer…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "GenerateVisitorToken" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Create a new visitor session and obtain a visitor access token for site "CodeStringers - Zoho Services" (https://www.codestringers.com/_api/mcp). You must use t…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "ExecuteWixAPI" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Run JavaScript against the Wix REST API on site "CodeStringers - Zoho Services" (https://www.codestringers.com/_api/mcp), on the visitor's behalf. The code runs…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "ExecuteWixAPI" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.
